title: Cacalia biafrae
taxonomy: Cacalia biafrae is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Cacalia, which is a group of flowering plants. Cacalia biafrae is further categorized under the subfamily Carduoideae. The exact classification can be broken down into the following taxonomic ranks: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Asterales, family Asteraceae, subfamily Carduoideae, tribe Cardueae, and finally the genus and species Cacalia biafrae. This classification is based on its morphological and genetic characteristics.
uses: Cacalia biafrae is used in traditional medicine due to its various health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may aid in the relief of pain and swelling. In some cultures, Cacalia biafrae is used to treat digestive issues and to boost the immune system. The plant is also thought to have antimicrobial properties, making it effective against certain types of infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of Cacalia biafrae.
